expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “Evidence-based confirmation (Phase 4b contract coverage) → 1) Case focus is explicitly covered”: it states the Phase 4b Contract purpose is to “Group the Phase 4a draft into the canonical top-layer taxonomy without merging away scenario granularity” and calls out an “Anti-Compression Rule” that “Do not merge distinct ... scenarios” and “forbids silently shrinking coverage.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4b
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “2) Output aligns with primary phase (phase4b)”: it lists Phase 4b entry/work/output artifacts and gates (e.g., “Entry: scripts/phase4b.sh”, “Output: phase4b_spawn_manifest.json”, “Required output artifact: drafts/qa_plan_phase4b_r<round>.md”, and post-gate validators including “coverage preservation against Phase 4a input” and “canonical layering”), asserting alignment to Phase 4b.
